title: Pro Padel League Raises $15 Million as Investors Bet on U.S. Growth
summary: The Pro Padel League closed a $15 million Series A round led by Charlotte Hornets co-chairman Rick Schnall and Left Lane Capital. The investment underscores growing institutional confidence in padel's trajectory as the fastest-growing racket sport in the United States.

title: Chingotto and Galán Claim Miami P1 Crown, Defeating Tapia and Coello
summary: Federico Chingotto and Alejandro Galán upset world No. 1 pair Agustín Tapia and Arturo Coello 7-5, 3-6, 6-3 to win the men's Miami P1 title. Chingotto was named MVP of the final after a masterful defensive display at the Miami Beach Convention Center.

title: González and Josemaría Win Miami P1 Title in Dramatic Final
summary: Bea González and Paula Josemaría captured their first Premier Padel P1 title in Miami, defeating top seeds Gemma Triay and Delfi Brea in a thrilling final at the Miami Beach Convention Center.

title: Agus Tapia's Impossible Drop Shot Stuns Miami Crowd
summary: Agustín Tapia produced one of the shots of the tournament during the Miami P1 semifinal, leaving fans — including tennis stars Sara Errani and Jasmine Paolini — in disbelief. The viral moment came as Tapia and Coello battled past Lebrón and Augsburger to reach the final.

title: Tapia and Coello Edge Past Lebrón and Augsburger in Thrilling Miami Semifinal
summary: Top seeds Tapia and Coello survived a scare from Lebrón and Augsburger, dropping the first set before storming back 5-7, 6-3, 6-2 to reach their 19th consecutive final on the Premier Padel tour.

title: Premier Padel Brings P1 Tournament to Miami Beach Convention Center
summary: Miami hosted its first Premier Padel P1 event March 22–30, drawing the world's top-ranked players and a $479,000 prize pool. The tournament featured 48 men's pairs and 28 women's pairs competing over nine days.

title: Pro Padel League Unveils 2026 Season With Five Events Across North America
summary: The PPL's 2026 season kicks off July 9 in New York City, followed by events in Los Angeles, Playa del Carmen, and Guadalajara before the inaugural City's Cup Finals in Miami. The league also launched PPL 2, a developmental circuit for North American talent.

title: 'State of Padel in the U.S.' Report Says Sport Has 'Irreversible Momentum'
summary: A new industry report projects 20,000 courts and 15 million active players in the U.S. by 2030. Player participation has already surged 250% since 2022, with over 70% of new sports facilities built in 2024 including padel courts.

title: Record-Breaking 2026 Tournament Schedule Signals a New Era for U.S. Padel
summary: The United States Padel Association announced 115 sanctioned tournaments for the first half of 2026 alone — the largest competitive calendar in American padel history. The expansion reflects surging demand across amateur and professional levels nationwide.

title: National Padel League Announces 2026 Season Across Six U.S. Cities
summary: The NPL will stage matches in Boston, Charlotte, San Diego, Dallas, Philadelphia, and Miami this season, featuring expanded women's divisions and a dual Spring/Fall competition format.
